title: Lessingianthus octanthus
intro: Lessingianthus octanthus, also known as the Brazilian yerba-santa, is a flowering plant species native to Brazil, characterized by its aromatic leaves and stems. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with health benefits including the reduction of inflammation and pain relief, as well as its potential to improve digestion and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Lessingianthus octanthus include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, often used to soothe respiratory issues and promote relaxation.
taxonomy: Lessingianthus octanthus is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is a member of the genus Lessingianthus, which is a group of flowering plants that are native to South America. The species is further classified as a type of daisy or composite flower, characterized by its yellow or orange daisy-like flowers and hairy leaves. Lessingianthus octanthus is native to the savannas and grasslands of Brazil and Argentina. This plant is often grown in gardens for its colorful flowers and ability to attract pollinators.
